title: TLS Clients and Servers
text: TLS Clients and Servers TLS and Uniface attach different meanings to the terms client and server . It is very important to understand TLS client and server roles when using certificate verification because it affects which TLS options are required. For Uniface, a server is a Uniface Server, or the remote machine on which both the Uniface Router and its Uniface Servers run. A client is a Uniface application that is not a Uniface Router or Uniface Server, or the machine on which this application runs. For TLS, a server is the application that creates a listening port and waits for connection requests. A TLS client is the application that initiates communication by connecting to a listening port. The TLS client can verify the certificate of the TLS server, and optionally, the TLS server can verify the validity of the certificate of the TLS client. This happens when the connection between the two is first established. In a Uniface environment, a Uniface application or Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) always has the role of TLS client, but Uniface Routers and Uniface Servers may function as both TLS servers and TLS clients: The Uniface Router is normally a TLS server. However, if URouter hopping is used, the Uniface Router that directs a request to another Uniface Router also functions as a TLS client. A Uniface Router in a chain therefore functions as both a TLS client of the next Uniface Router in the chain, and a TLS server of the previous Uniface Router in the chain. All Uniface Servers are initially TLS clients because they have to register with the Uniface Router. Shared Uniface Servers remain TLS clients, but exclusive Uniface Servers become TLS servers when they create a listening port for the client application to connect to. The WRD functions as a TLS client. Related Links Logical and Physical Connections When a client application needs to connect to a Uniface Server, it does so via a Uniface Router, which makes the required physical connections from the client to the Uniface Server. The client may have multiple logical connections and each logical connection may pass over several physical connections. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
